pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi argues that the only reliable path from bad to good is a willingness to be bad — and to remain in that bad state — for a very long time. He cites Charlie Munger's principle: in order to get what you want, you must deserve what you want, which means becoming genuinely good, and the only way to become good is sustained bad-period tolerance.

PayPal's two-sided referral program ($10 credit to both referrer and referee) helped them reach 1 million users within 2 years and 100 million users within 6 years of launch.

pairs.a_topic: AI as Execution Leverage, Not Business Model Shift
pairs.a_text: Alex Hormozi asserts that AI will not change the fundamental rules of business (what makes a good offer, what drives sales, how organizations scale) but will change the tools available to competitors, increasing output leverage — and frames the transition as man-plus-better-tools vs. man-plus-better-tools, the same competitive dynamic that has always existed.
